input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Good — both .NET 9 and 10 runtimes are available. Let me read the source files.
Now I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that multi-targets .NET 9 and .NET 10.
data.reasoningText: Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that targets both net9.0 and net10.0, benchmarking the three methods. Let me create a benchmark project.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-7679d13aca5b4e49aaacaced7d46664c/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-7679d13aca5b4e49aaacaced7d46664c/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-7679d13aca5b4e49aaacaced7d46664c/Benchmarks/Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-7679d13aca5b4e49aaacaced7d46664c/Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net10.0 -- --runtimes net9.0 net10.0 --join 2>&1 | tail -80

data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet needs to be run from one framework, and it will handle multi-targeting itself. Let me run it from net10.0 and configure it to benchmark both runtimes.

Actually, BenchmarkDotNet's multi-targeting works differently. I need to add `[SimpleJob]` attributes for each runtime, or use a config. Let me update the code to use the `--runtimes` CLI argument or add job attributes.

The simplest approach: run with `--framework net10.0` and use `--runtimes net90 net100` CLI args.

Actually, BenchmarkDotNet uses `net90` and `net100` as runtime monikers. 

Let me verify the correct runtime identifiers for BenchmarkDotNet. I think the recent versions use `net9.0` and `net10.0` format, so I'll try running with those as the runtime arguments instead.
